sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root לָקַח)-

(1) prey, booty, but especially used of cattle. Num 31:12, אֶת־הַשְּׁבִי וְאֶת־הַמַּלְקחַ וְאֶת־הַשָּׁלָל “captives, and booty, and spoil;” but verse 11 Num 31:11, 27 Num 31:27, 32 Num 31:32, captives are included also in this word, Isa 49:24, 25 Isa 49:25.

(2) Dual מַלְקֹוחַיִם both jaws, by which food is taken, Psa 22:16.
